steps.title: Gather Supplies Scalpels Glass slides 14...
steps.instruction: Gather Supplies Scalpels Glass slides 14 mL Dounce 20 µm vacuum filter Eppendorf tubes (1.5 mL and 5 mL) Eppendorf or Rainin pipette tips Dry ice Metal plate OCT (Optimal cutting temperature compound) RNAse free water Cell counting supplies (LUNA-FL) Other Reagents: PBS BSA RNAse inhibitor (i) Nuclei EZ lysis buffer (NUC201-1KT) OptiPrep Density Gradient Medium (60% Iodixanol - G60) (ab286850) Stock solutions: 500 mM tricine : 8.96 g of tricine in 100 mL water 1 M KCl : 7.45 g of KCl in 100 mL water 1 M MgCl2x6H2O : 20.3 g of MgCl2x6H2O in 100 mL water

steps.title: Optiprep Buffers (can be made in...
steps.instruction: Optiprep Buffers (can be made in advance and kept on hand) %s %s GDGHComponentVolumeComponentVolume500 mM Tricine24 mLSucrose8.5 g1M KCl15 mLWater50 mL1M MgCl2 x 6H2O3 mL500 mM Tricine4 mLWater50 mLKCl2.5 mL1M KOHAdjust to pH 7.81M MgCl2x6H2O0.5 mLBring volume up to 100 mL with water (keep at room temp)1M KOHAdjust to pH 7.8Bring volume up to 100 mL with water (keep at 4°C)

steps.title: Turn on and chill centrifuge to...
steps.instruction: Turn on and chill centrifuge to 4℃ Prepare all the reagents needed on ice Clean glass slides with ethanol Gather the dounce, pestles, and scalpels on ice

steps.title: Solutions to make fresh before starting...
steps.instruction: Solutions to make fresh before starting experiment Nuclei EZ lysis buffer Pour 12 mL of buffer into the dounce and reserve 1 mL for wetting down the sides of the dounce PBSA + i (1% BSA and 1 U/µL RNase inhibitor in 1X PBS) For powder BSA, add 0.01 g of BSA per 1 mL of 1X PBS G30: (Mix the G60 and GD together thoroughly before adding the GH) %s %s G30PBSA + iNuclei EZ Lysis BufferG60 (OptiPrep)6.0 mLPBS (pH 7.4) (1X)1 mL of 10X PBSEZ Lysis Buffer12,975 µLGD1.2 mLBSA (100X) 1 mL of 10% BSARNAse Inhibitor325 µLGH4.8 mLRNase Inhibitor (200X) 50 µLTotal 12 mLRNase free water7,950 µL

steps.title: Wet a 20 µm vacuum filter...
steps.instruction: Wet a 20 µm vacuum filter with 1 mL of PBSA + i

steps.title: On a glass slide on dry...
steps.instruction: On a glass slide on dry ice , shave off pieces of tissue with a scalpel until you have 50±3 mg of tissue

steps.title: For managing tissue, use OCT to...
steps.instruction: For managing tissue, use OCT to adhere tissue to glass slide

steps.title: Transfer all tissue to dounce filled...
steps.instruction: Transfer all tissue to dounce filled with Nuclei EZ Lysis Buffer

steps.title: Gently dounce tissue on ice 20...
steps.instruction: Gently dounce tissue on ice 20 times with pestle “A” then 20 times with pestle “B” until the tissue is homogenized

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 10 minutes

steps.title: Use 1mL of the reserved Nuclei...
steps.instruction: Use 1mL of the reserved Nuclei EZ Lysis Buffer to wet down the side of the dounce if tissue is stuck on the side of the dounce

steps.title: Place tissue pieces on a boat on dry ice
steps.instruction: Place tissue pieces on a boat on dry ice. Hold the tissue on dry ice until all tissue has been cut

steps.title: Vacuum filter the homogenized tissue
steps.instruction: Vacuum filter the homogenized tissue

steps.title: Transfer the homogenized tissue to (3) 5 mL tubes
steps.instruction: Transfer the homogenized tissue to (3) 5 mL tubes

steps.title: If the filter clogs, remove any...
steps.instruction: If the filter clogs, remove any unfiltered volume with a pipette, replace the filter, and continue filtering

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 4℃ for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 4℃ for 5 min (500 x g)

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant (be...
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant (be careful not to disturb cell pellets) and resuspend each pellet in 900 µL G30

steps.title: Alternatively, split pellet into (9) 1
steps.instruction: Alternatively, split pellet into (9) 1.5 mL tubes (total), resuspend each pellet in 300 µL of G30, then underlay each sample with 1 mL of G30

steps.title: Gently underlay each 5mL tube with...
steps.instruction: Gently underlay each 5mL tube with 3 mL G30 Place the pipette tip filled with G30 at the bottom of the 5mL tube. Slowly dispense G30 underneath the cell suspension. A clear separation will form between homogenate and G30 (cloudy on top, clear on the bottom). Do not disturb the separation between the layers

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 4℃ for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 4℃ for 10 min (4,255 x g)

steps.title: For capturing smaller cell types, spin...
steps.instruction: For capturing smaller cell types, spin at 8,000 x g for 20 minutes

steps.title: Remove and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Remove the top (very viscous) layer...
steps.instruction: Remove the top (very viscous) layer from the tube first- this layer contains fats and must be removed before removing the rest of the supernatant

steps.title: Resuspend the pellets in 1 mL PBSA + i
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellets in 1 mL PBSA + i

steps.title: Centrifuge the samples at 4℃ for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the samples at 4℃ for 5 min (500 x g) (single wash)

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the pellets in 200 - 1,000 µL of PBSA + i (depending on the size of the pellet)

steps.title: Count nuclei using LUNA-FL counter
steps.instruction: Count nuclei using LUNA-FL counter

steps.title: Optional if you have excess nuclei,...
steps.instruction: Optional if you have excess nuclei, or if you need to pause before continuing.

steps.title: Centrifuge the nuclei at 4℃ for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the nuclei at 4℃ for 5 min (500 x g)

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the nuclei in CryoStor (CryoStor® CS10), mix by pipetting

steps.title: ≤ 1k nuclei / µL of CryoStor
steps.instruction: ≤ 1k nuclei / µL of CryoStor

steps.title: Transfer the nuclei to cryotubes and...
steps.instruction: Transfer the nuclei to cryotubes and store them in a Mr Frosty in a -80℃ freezer until frozen (1 day)

steps.title: For long term storage, transfer cryotubes...
steps.instruction: For long term storage, transfer cryotubes to regular storage in a -80℃ freezer

steps.title: Thaw the CryoStored nuclei
steps.instruction: Thaw the CryoStored nuclei

steps.title: Centrifuge the nuclei at 4°C for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the nuclei at 4°C for 10 min (1200 x g)

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend in 1mL PBSA + i
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend in 1mL PBSA + i

steps.title: Centrifuge the nuclei at 4°C for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the nuclei at 4°C for 5 min (500 x g) (single wash)

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the pelleted nuclei in PBSA + i

steps.title: Count the nuclei before proceeding to next steps
steps.instruction: Count the nuclei before proceeding to next steps
